title: Unchecked unwrap() calls could panic in build functions
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs

Multiple build functions use .unwrap() on Vec::push/extend_from_slice operations:

Line 469-481 (Icmpv6Echo::build):
```rust
pkt.push(msg_type).unwrap();
pkt.push(0).unwrap();
// ... more unwraps
```

Line 517-527 (NeighborSolicitation::build):
```rust
pkt.push(icmpv6_type::NEIGHBOR_SOLICITATION).unwrap();
pkt.push(0).unwrap();
// ... more unwraps
```

Line 569-587 (NeighborAdvertisement::build):
```rust
pkt.push(icmpv6_type::NEIGHBOR_ADVERTISEMENT).unwrap();
// ... more unwraps
```

While the comments claim these 'cannot fail' because capacity was checked, the code uses unwrap() rather than:
1. unsafe { pkt.push_unchecked() } (if truly guaranteed)
2. Proper error propagation

In debug builds, if the capacity calculation is ever wrong, these will panic rather than return an error.

Recommendation: Either:
- Use expect() with a message explaining why it cannot fail
- Use unwrap_unchecked() in unsafe block (documents the invariant)
- Propagate errors properly

This is a code quality issue rather than a functional bug, but panics in no_std environments are problematic.
